_note: TEST ALPHA-2 - CLEAN PAINTED MISS (selector paints the TYPE, pre-clamp zeroes the DAMAGE). test-alpha proved the split: forcing evade-type +0x1C0=0x06 made the record show a miss, but damage lives in SEPARATE fields (+0x1C4=184 staged, +0x1E5 high-bit=apply) and still applied (Ramza 567 -> 383 each hit). So a clean miss needs BOTH levers: (1) selector forces +0x1C0=0x06 (miss animation/type) - unchanged from test-alpha; (2) pre-clamp 0x30A66F forces the applied debit to 0 on Ramza. Together = miss animation + 0 damage. Floor unchanged: force-hit (evade 0 broadcast) + Brave 10 (Blade Grasp suppressed) so every attack is a natural hit to repaint. RUN: relaunch, attack Ramza from the front, physical melee, 4-5 times. OBSERVE PER ATTACK: (a) does Ramza show a MISS / dodge (not a hit)? (b) does his HP stay the same (0 damage)? PASS = dodge + 0 HP loss on a guaranteed hit = OUTPUT fully controls the result (type via selector + magnitude via pre-clamp); the miss/block/parry question is SOLVED via output + input-floor. The log shows [SELECTOR-PROBE ... CONTROL WROTE evadeType=0x06] AND a [PRECLAMP-REWRITE ...] line forcing debit 0; the ACTION-STATE poller shows Ramza's HP flat. If HP still drops -> the 184 reaches HP via a path the pre-clamp misses; next step is to extend the selector control to zero +0x1C4/+0x1E5 in place. Safe: only Ramza painted + zeroed; struct writes only.
